QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_10

question.text: Mr. Harish filed his income tax return for FY 2025-26 on 15th January 2027, which is after the due date under Section 139(1). He had a non-speculative business loss of Rs.3,00,000 and unabsorbed depreciation of Rs.2,00,000. Which of these can he carry forward?

answer.text: Both business loss and unabsorbed depreciation can be carried forward
Neither can be carried forward since the return was filed late
Only unabsorbed depreciation of Rs.2,00,000 can be carried forward; business loss lapses
Only business loss of Rs.3,00,000 can be carried forward; unabsorbed depreciation lapses

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 80, carry forward of business loss (non-speculative and speculative) and capital loss is allowed ONLY if the return is filed on or before the due date under Section 139(1). However, unabsorbed depreciation (Section 32(2)) and house property loss are NOT subject to this condition — they can be carried forward even if the return is filed late. **The Trap:** Option A ignores the due date filing requirement for business losses. Option D reverses the rule entirely. **Cross-Exam Context:** This filing deadline condition is a critical exam point across NISM Series X-A and X-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always remind business clients: file before the due date. A late return means permanent loss of the right to carry forward business and capital losses — potentially lakhs in future tax savings gone.
